input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"
    Title="MyMauiApp"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Dashboard — single page, own flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products — flyout item with two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="products.png">
        <Tab Title="Active">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
        </Tab>
        <Tab Title="Archived">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings — own flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to show them how to set up a .NET MAUI AppShell.xaml with a specific structure. This is a code generation/teaching request - I don't need to explore any codebase. Let me create the files directly.

Structure needed:
1. Flyout with "Dashboard" section (single page)
2. "Products" section with two tabs: "Active" and "Archived"
3. Settings page accessible from flyout
4. Route registration for ProductDetailsPage

Let me create the AppShell.xaml and AppShell.xaml.cs files.

<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"

    xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"
    Title="MyMauiApp"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Dashboard — single page, own flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products — flyout item with two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="products.png">
        <Tab Title="Active">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
        </Tab>
        <Tab Title="Archived">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings — own flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.content:
Here's the structure breakdown:

**`AppShell.xaml`** — Three `FlyoutItem` entries:

| Flyout entry | Content |
|---|---|
| **Dashboard** | Single `ShellContent` → `DashboardPage` |
| **Products** | Two `Tab`s → `ActiveProductsPage` / `ArchivedProductsPage` |
| **Settings** | Single `ShellContent` → `SettingsPage` |

**`AppShell.xaml.cs`** — Registers `ProductDetailsPage` as a non-visual route so you can push to it from either product list:

```csharp
// From ActiveProductsPage or ArchivedProductsPage:
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"{nameof(ProductDetailsPage)}?id={product.Id}");
```

To accept the query parameter, add `[QueryProperty("Id", "id")]` (or the `IQueryAttributable` interface) to your `ProductDetailsPage` or its view-model.

**Pages you still need to create** under a `Pages/` folder:
`DashboardPage`, `ActiveProductsPage`, `ArchivedProductsPage`, `SettingsPage`, `ProductDetailsPage`.
